records.ety: [F. sanctifier, L. sanctificare; sanctus holy + -ficare (in comp.) to make. See Saint, and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sanctified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sanctifying (?).]

defs: To make sacred or holy; to set apart to a holy or religious use; to consecrate by appropriate rites; to hallow.
quotes: God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it.
Moses . . . sanctified Aaron and his garments.

defs: To make free from sin; to cleanse from moral corruption and pollution; to purify.
To make efficient as the means of holiness; to render productive of holiness or piety.
quotes: A means which his mercy hath sanctified so to me as to make me repent of that unjust act.

defs: To impart or impute sacredness, venerableness, inviolability, title to reverence and respect, or the like, to; to secure from violation; to give sanction to.
quotes: The holy man, amazed at what he saw, Made haste to sanctify the bliss by law.
Truth guards the poet, sanctifies the line.
